\boxed{B}
**Clinical Reasoning:** There is no mention in the provided evidence linking shaving the incision site to an increased infection rate after spinal surgery. Standard preventive practices emphasize skin antisepsis (e.g., chlorhexidine) prior to procedures, rather than hair removal. Given that infection risks are already minimized by strict sterilization techniques and prophylaxis, there is insufficient data suggesting shaving contributes to higher infection rates. Thus, shaving is unlikely to increase infection risk.
